content.text:
Parent conversation handoff summary:
Parent conversation handoff summary
Summary model: openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini
Source items: 73

Overview:
The discussion centered on whether LightRAG-style simplification would fit this repo’s AI stack, then shifted into a broader AI pipeline optimization effort. The repo already has a fairly advanced RAG/AI architecture: Spring Boot backend, Pinecone vector search, prompt templates, eval harnesses, and derived read models. The resulting direction was to keep a reusable experiment framework, on-demand A/B evaluation, and a few quality/cost improvements, all with backward-compatible defaults.

Settled decisions:
- Proceed with a reusable live experiment framework, not just one-off eval scripts.
- Keep experiments default-off so current behavior is unchanged unless explicitly enabled.
- Use OpenRouter as the sole LLM gateway; do not add direct provider calls.
- Use existing eval datasets/judging infrastructure for on-demand A/B comparisons.
- Record experiment/model metadata in existing audit-related fields rather than adding schema migrations.
- Add prompt caching support for Anthropic models via OpenRouter.
- Add SQL routing for analytical questions to use read models instead of vector search.
- Keep HyDE as an opt-in, experiment-controlled RAG enhancement.
- Add chunk-size monitoring/analysis as a diagnostic/observability improvement.
- A plan file was written: `duet-plan-ai-optimization.md` for duet-based implementation.

Constraints / must-remember items:
- Implementation should stay backend-only for this plan; no frontend/package/e2e changes.
- Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.4 stack; avoid introducing new framework dependencies.
- All new behaviors must be backward compatible and disabled by default.
- OpenRouter remains the only model gateway.
- Existing eval tests must continue to pass.
- The plan assumes no database schema changes.
- The repo already contains derived read models relevant to SQL routing: UserWeekFact, TeamWeekRollup, RcdoWeekRollup, CarryForwardFact, ComplianceFact.
- Duet config in `.pi/duet/config.json` shows static checks: `npm run check:static`; unit checks: `npm test`.
- There is already an in-progress duet run with modified files under `.pi/duet/runs/2026-03-29T18-32-40-613Z-32faa966/` and tracked changes in `AiController` plus new plan recommendation files.

Open questions:
- Should the initial implementation keep the full live traffic experiment service, or trim it down later if it proves too heavy?
- Which exact models should be treated as control/treatment defaults for LLM and embeddings in the reusable A/B harness?
- Should analytical SQL routing be limited to clearly aggregate/comparison queries, or should it also handle broader reporting-style questions?
- How much observability is wanted for prompt caching and chunk-size analysis beyond basic logging/counters?
- Should the A/B runner compare only LLMs initially, or also embeddings and HyDE in the same reusable harness?

Use this handoff as background context. If it conflicts with the explicit task below or the current repository state, prioritize the explicit task and repo state.

Plan overview:
{
  "goal": "Add seven optimization capabilities to the AI pipeline — prompt caching, A/B testing infrastructure, embedding model override (offline eval only), LLM model A/B, HyDE, SQL query routing, and chunk size analysis — all backward-compatible and default-off. The embedding-model A/B experiment is scoped to offline evaluation only (via AbComparisonRunner) because the Pinecone index is fixed at 1536 dimensions matching text-embedding-3-small; routing live queries through text-embedding-3-large (3072-d) would cause dimension mismatches. Backend-only; no frontend, e2e, or schema changes.",
  "totalSteps": 7,
  "stepTitles": [
    "1. Prompt Caching in OpenRouterAiProvider",
    "2. A/B Testing Infrastructure",
    "3. Embedding Model Override (Offline Eval) + Chunk Size Analysis",
    "4. LLM Model A/B + Experiment-Aware Provider",
    "5. HyDE Service + SQL Query Router",
    "6. Wire HyDE, SQL Routing, and LLM A/B into SemanticQueryService",
    "7. A/B Comparison Eval Runner"
  ]
}

Step 7: A/B Comparison Eval Runner
{
  "id": "step-7",
  "title": "A/B Comparison Eval Runner",
  "description": "Add an offline A/B comparison runner for evaluating model and embedding variants. (1) Create backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/eval/AbComparisonRunner.java — @Tag(\"ab-eval\") separate from normal eval and test runs. Reads same golden datasets as PromptEvalRunner. For each case, runs against two LLM models (AB_CONTROL_MODEL and AB_TREATMENT_MODEL env vars, defaults: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4 and google/gemini-2.5-flash-preview). Creates two OpenRouterAiProvider instances, one per model. Compares schema validity, judge scores, latency, token usage. Also supports AB_CONTROL_EMBEDDING and AB_TREATMENT_EMBEDDING for offline embedding model comparison (creates separate EmbeddingService instances per model, embeds test queries, compares resulting vectors for retrieval quality — this is the correct place for embedding model A/B since it doesn't touch the live 1536-d Pinecone index). Writes comparison report to build/eval-results/ab-comparison-{timestamp}.json with per-case scores and aggregate summary (mean score, win rate, statistical summary). (2) Modify backend/build.gradle.kts — add abEvalTest task: tasks.register<Test>(\"abEvalTest\") with includeTags(\"ab-eval\"), 300s timeout, forwarding OPENROUTER_API_KEY and AB_* env vars. Also update the main test task's excludeTags to include \"ab-eval\" alongside existing \"eval\" exclusion.",
  "requiredChecks": [
    "static"
  ]
}

This is relay round 2. Another agent has already worked on this step.

You are in VERIFICATION MODE. Your default action is to APPROVE.
Focus your review on the DIFF — the changes made in the previous round.
Only make changes if there is a MATERIAL defect: failing check, missing step requirement, logic bug, or security issue.
Style preferences, alternative approaches, comment wording, and optional polish are NOT grounds for changes.

If all checks pass and the step requirements are met, approve immediately.
Log anything else you notice as observations — do not act on them.

Previous agent's notes:
Everything looks correct. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## Summary

### `AbComparisonRunner.java` (new)

`@Tag("ab-eval") @TestInstance(PER_CLASS)` class in `com.weeklycommit.ai.eval` package.

**Setup (`@BeforeAll`):**
- Reads `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` (skips gracefully if absent)
- Reads `AB_CONTROL_MODEL` (default: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4`), `AB_TREATMENT_MODEL` (default: `google/gemini-2.5-flash-preview`)
- Reads `AB_CONTROL_EMBEDDING` (default: `openai/text-embedding-3-small`), `AB_TREATMENT_EMBEDDING` (default: `openai/text-embedding-3-large`)
- Creates two separate `OpenRouterAiProvider` instances (one per LLM model) and two `EmbeddingService` instances (one per embedding model)

**`@Test runLlmAbComparison()`:**
- Loops over `commit-draft-assist` and `rag-query` golden datasets
- For each case, invokes both control and treatment providers, records: schema validity, latency, token delta (via `getTotalTokensUsed()` before/after), winner determination (schema first, latency as tiebreak)

**`@Test runEmbeddingAbComparison()`:**
- Derives test queries from rag-query cases (falls back to hardcoded defaults)
- Calls both `EmbeddingService` instances for each query, records: dimensions, success, latency, L2 magnitude
- Correctly notes that cross-dimension cosine similarity is not computed, and treatment is offline-only

**`@AfterAll writeReport()`:**
- Writes `build/eval-results/ab-comparison-{timestamp}.json` with per-case details and aggregate summary (schema pass rate, win rate, mean latency, total tokens, success rates)
- Logs a readable summary to stdout

### `build.gradle.kts` changes
1. Updated `test` task's `excludeTags` from `"eval"` to `"eval", "ab-eval"` — ensures `AbComparisonRunner` never runs in normal CI
2. Added new `abEvalTest` task: `includeTags("ab-eval")`, 300s timeout, forwards `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` and all `AB_*` env vars

**Checks:** `static` ✅, `unit` ✅ (all 22 JS + all Java tests pass; `ab-eval` correctly excluded from `./gradlew test`)

content.text:
You are evaluating the faithfulness of an AI-generated answer in a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system.

Faithfulness measures whether every factual claim in the answer can be directly attributed to the retrieved context. An unfaithful answer contains hallucinated information not present in the context.

## Answer to evaluate:
"{answer}"

## Retrieved context chunks:
{retrievedChunks}

## Instructions:
1. Decompose the answer into individual factual claims (atomic statements).
2. For each claim, determine if it is SUPPORTED (directly inferable from a retrieved chunk) or UNSUPPORTED (not found in any chunk).
3. Calculate the faithfulness score as: (supported claims) / (total claims).

Respond with ONLY a JSON object:
{
  "claims": [
    {"claim": "the atomic statement", "supported": true, "sourceChunkId": "chunk ID or null"},
    {"claim": "another statement", "supported": false, "sourceChunkId": null}
  ],
  "faithfulnessScore": 0.85,
  "unsupportedClaims": ["list of claims not found in context"]
}

content.thinking:
**Considering implementation strategies**

I may need to inspect the structure of other cases, but if I implement validators similarly, it might not be necessary. Since this is just verification, I could note material defects without fixing them. However, the instructions say to fix defects directly, so I should attempt that. I also need to find the current class's compile and style, implement judge scoring, and all datasets. I believe I can do this in time. I might create helper methods for schema validation based on the PromptEvalRunner.

**Exploring judge scores and retrieval quality**

The next step is to compare judge scores, and I think one judge dimension should suffice. However, some datasets may not have existing judge infrastructure, leading to judge scores being null. We need optional judge scores for commit-draft and rag-query, at least. I can aggregate the mean judge score over the cases that have scores. Now, regarding embedding retrieval quality, it's more challenging since there's no index touch. I could compute proxy retrieval quality comparing query vectors with vectors from rag-query cases. I'll check if the top-K retrieved matches the expected order of `retrievedChunks`.

**Evaluating retrieval quality**

I think I can create offline embeddings for queries and their retrieved chunk texts, then compute cosine similarities to see if the top-ranked chunks support the expected behavior keywords. Without negative samples in the corpus, the retrieval quality could be measured by the best chunk score among the expected keyword chunks or using MRR based on relevant chunks. I’ll stick to the rag-query golden dataset since it has the retrieved chunks and expected keywords. For each query, I’ll embed both the query and chunk texts, check for top-ranked relevant chunks, and compute MRR or recall@k for quality assessment.

**Evaluating AI context types**

I’m questioning whether I need to inspect all AI context types. It seems possible there might be a bug when loading cases, where the stream isn’t closing the input stream properly. I think about computing a statistical summary, maybe calculating deltas and standard deviation could suffice. The step mentions mean scores and win rates, so I should include those along with counts of wins and ties. This is round 2 of verification, so I might only need minimal fixes for now.

**Modifying AbComparisonRunner**

I’m planning to modify the AbComparisonRunner to add dataset specifications for the same datasets as the PromptEvalRunner, like commit-draft-assist and others. For each spec, I’ll include resource paths, suggestion types, context builders, and optionally a schema validator or judge scorer function. The runLlmAbComparison will loop through all specs and cases, calling providers and validating schemas. I’ll determine the winner based on schema validity, judge scores, and latency. Summaries need to include various metrics like mean judge scores and potential win margins.

**Inspecting static problems**

I’m checking for potential static problems. I think the loadCases method returns a Stream, and since streams can only be used once, that’s something to keep in mind. I'll consider using TypeReference<List<EvalCase>> to avoid raw types. Since this file is new, it might be easier to rewrite the whole thing rather than edit it directly. content.text:
Reviewed the Step 7 diff in:

- `backend/build.gradle.kts`
- `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/eval/AbComparisonRunner.java`

I found material gaps in the new runner, so I fixed them directly.

What I changed:
- Expanded `AbComparisonRunner` to read the same golden datasets used by `PromptEvalRunner`, not just a subset:
  - commit-draft-assist
  - commit-lint
  - rcdo-suggest
  - risk-signal
  - reconcile-assist
  - rag-query
  - what-if
- Added judge-score comparison support:
  - commit-draft-assist reuses the existing title/criteria judge prompts
  - rag-query reuses the faithfulness judge prompt
- Improved winner selection to use:
  - schema validity first
  - judge score when available
  - latency as tiebreaker
- Upgraded embedding A/B from dimension/magnitude-only reporting to actual offline retrieval-quality comparison using rag-query fixtures:
  - cosine-similarity ranking
  - MRR
  - recall@3
- Added aggregate/statistical summary fields to the report for both LLM and embedding comparisons.
- Fixed a runtime defect in report generation where `Map.of(...)` could throw on null summary values.
- Fixed embedding latency measurement so it reflects the full offline retrieval-quality evaluation path, not just the initial query embedding.

What I verified:
- `build.gradle.kts` correctly excludes `ab-eval` from normal `test`
- `abEvalTest` exists, includes only `ab-eval`, uses 300s timeout, and forwards the required env vars
- `abEvalTest` runs successfully without an API key and skips gracefully

Checks:
- `npm run check:static` ✅
- `npm test` ✅
- `cd backend && ./gradlew abEvalTest --no-daemon` ✅

Assessment:
- The original round had missing Step 7 functionality.
- After these fixes, the step requirements are met and checks pass.

```observations
- [low] frontend/src/components/myweek/PlanHistoryView.tsx: Existing test output still reports a missing React `key` warning.
- [low] frontend/src/components/notifications/NotificationPanel.tsx: Existing test output still reports repeated React `act(...)` warnings.
```

Verdict: changes_made
